mission: Awwwards June 2026 — Unique Design System, Hero & Navigation
target: Awwwards SOTD quality — restraint, scroll-driven narrative, kinetic type, custom GLSL, CIS-adapted luxury

awwwards_intel.source: Awwwards SOTD June 2026 (Son Daven, Steven.com, Truck'N Roll, Apechain, Hubtown, Serve Robotics), Svilenković 2026 3D analysis, Coalition Technologies 2026 trends

key_trends_june_2026.restraint: Winners commit to ONE strong visual concept — not mixed effects. 2024's 'more is more' is dead.
key_trends_june_2026.scroll_narrative: Scrollytelling outscores static 3D by 1.8 points. Adaptive pacing is #1 creativity predictor.
key_trends_june_2026.kinetic_type: Variable fonts + animated type as storytelling. Oversized headlines as visual anchors.
key_trends_june_2026.custom_shaders: 78% of top scorers use custom GLSL. Generic particle backgrounds are saturated.
key_trends_june_2026.dark_light_contrast: 67% of 2026 winners use dark hero → light sections transition.
key_trends_june_2026.performance_budget: Lighthouse mobile 90+ is baseline. 60fps sustained is hidden rubric.
key_trends_june_2026.accessibility: prefers-reduced-motion respected by all SOTD winners in 2026.
key_trends_june_2026.bento_grids: Asymmetric Bento layouts replace identical card grids.
key_trends_june_2026.micro_interactions: Subtle hover states, button feedback, scroll-triggered state changes.
key_trends_june_2026.tldr_content: Concise, scan-first. 1-2 lines per headline, visual storytelling over text blocks.

what_loses_2026: Generic particle backgrounds (sin/cos drift is recognizable)

judge_criteria.design: 40% — spatial composition, lighting hierarchy, material-based brand identity

tasks.title: Unique Design System — Anti-Slop Identity + Token Expansion
tasks.description: Audit and expand the existing token system to create a truly unique visual identity. Key actions: (1) Add blob animation tokens (@property --blob-x/y) for organic anti-grid backgrounds. (2) Add mesh-gradient tokens for hero vignette. (3) Verify gold accent tokens exist and are used sparingly (≤5% surface). (4) Remove any remaining gradient-text usage. (5) Restrict glassmorphism to hero section only. (6) Add signature visual pattern — a unique button shape or corner radius that repeats across the site for brand memory. (7) Document the unique identity rationale in docs/design-system.md.
deliverables: src/app/[locale]/tokens.css — expanded with blob, mesh-gradient, signature-pattern tokens
src/app/[locale]/globals.css — removed gradient-text, restricted glassmorphism, added blob keyframes
acceptance_criteria: Glassmorphism restricted to hero section only
Gold accent used on ≤5% of total surface area
Signature visual pattern defined and applied to buttons/cards

tasks.title: Three.js — Simplex Noise Particles + Volumetric Light Rays + Performance Budget
tasks.description: Upgrade ThreeBackground from basic sin/cos drift to 2026 SOTD-level effects: (1) Replace sin/cos particle drift with simplex noise (3D) for organic, non-repetitive motion. (2) Add volumetric light ray shader emerging from bottom-left corner (emerald tint, 5% opacity). (3) Add second particle layer — foreground sparkles (200 particles) for depth parallax. (4) Implement PerformanceBudget class: monitors frame time, dynamically reduces shader complexity/particle count if frame time exceeds 16.6ms. (5) Target: 60fps sustained on M1/Ryzen 5, 45fps+ on Pixel 6/iPhone 13. (6) Adaptive: 500 particles on low-end, 2500 on high-end desktop.
deliverables: src/lib/three/PerformanceBudget.ts — adaptive quality by frame time
acceptance_criteria: Volumetric light ray shader from bottom-left (emerald, 5% opacity)
Two particle layers: background (800-2000) + foreground sparkles (100-300)
PerformanceBudget monitors frame time, reduces quality if >16.6ms
prefers-reduced-motion: graceful fallback to CSS particles (30 dust motes)

tasks.title: Hero Section — Cinematic 2026 Redesign with Scroll-Driven Camera
tasks.description: Complete Hero redesign matching June 2026 SOTD quality: (1) Implement scroll-driven camera zoom — Three.js camera z: 50→45 over 500px scroll. (2) Upgrade TextRevealWords: add per-character mode, delayed blur-in (8px→0) matching Apple keynote style. (3) Replace trust badge row with animated cert badges — staggered entrance translateX(60px)→0 + scale(0.7)→1 + rotate(8deg)→0. (4) Add mesh-gradient background layer behind Three.js (3 radial gradients, emerald 4% opacity). (5) Implement cursor-proximity glow: subtle emerald glow follows cursor within hero bounds. (6) Mobile: simplified reveal, no blur, 300 particles, no parallax. (7) Remove FloatingCards — they're a 2024 pattern, too cluttered for 2026 restraint principle.
acceptance_criteria: Scroll-driven Three.js camera zoom: z 50→45 over 500px scroll
TextRevealWords: blur-in (8px→0) + optional char mode, 50ms stagger
Cert badges: staggered entrance with rotation + scale
Mesh-gradient: 3 radial gradients, emerald 4%, behind Three.js canvas
prefers-reduced-motion: all animations disabled, content visible
LCP < 1.5s (hero content visible before Three.js loads)

tasks.title: Navigation — Morphing Logo + Active Section Progress + Premium Micro-interactions
tasks.description: Upgrade Navbar to 2026 premium standard: (1) Implement morphing logo animation on scroll — SVG path interpolation from full 'M-International' mark to compact 'M' monogram. (2) Active section progress indicator: thin emerald line below nav showing current section position (IntersectionObserver). (3) Desktop nav links: hover reveals animated underline that fills left-to-right (0.3s cubic-bezier). (4) Mobile menu: full-screen overlay with staggered link entrance (translateY(40px)→0, opacity 0→1, 80ms stagger), subtle CSS particle pattern background. (5) Scroll-to-top button appears after 800px scroll with spring animation. (6) Transparent → solid background on scroll (0.3s, no layout shift). (7) No glassmorphism when scrolled — solid bg only.
deliverables: src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx — full upgrade with morphing logo
acceptance_criteria: Morphing logo: SVG path animation full mark → monogram on scroll, 0.3s
Active section indicator: emerald line, IntersectionObserver, smooth updates
Nav link hover: left-to-right underline fill, 0.3s cubic-bezier
Mobile menu: full-screen, link stagger (translateY→0, opacity→1, 80ms gap)
Mobile menu background: subtle CSS particle pattern (no Three.js)
Scroll-to-top: appears at 800px, spring animation, 44px touch target
Transparent → solid background on scroll (0.3s, no layout shift)
prefers-reduced-motion: morphing logo = instant, menu = instant

tasks.title: Scroll Narrative — GSAP Master Timeline + Adaptive Pacing + Parallax Layers
tasks.description: Build a master scroll timeline that orchestrates the entire page narrative: (1) GSAP ScrollTrigger master timeline mapping scroll position (0→1) to section transitions. (2) Adaptive pacing: fast scroll = abbreviated transitions, slow scroll = full animation (strongest creativity predictor per 2026 research). (3) 3-layer parallax per section: bg 0.9x, content 1x, foreground 1.1x scroll speed. (4) Section entrance animations: alternating left/right reveals (Apple-style). (5) Section transitions: subtle crossfade + translate, NOT snap-scroll (CIS prohibition). (6) Lenis smooth scroll on desktop (≥768px), disabled on mobile.
deliverables: src/lib/scroll/MasterTimeline.ts — GSAP master timeline, scroll position 0→1 mapping
src/lib/scroll/AdaptivePacing.ts — fast/slow scroll detection, transition scaling
src/components/ui/ScrollReveal.tsx — upgraded with GSAP master timeline integration
acceptance_criteria: GSAP master timeline: scroll position 0→1 mapped to full page narrative
Adaptive pacing: transitions scale by scroll velocity
3-layer parallax per section: bg 0.9x, content 1x, foreground 1.1x
Section entrances: alternating left/right reveals, 0.7s, power3.out
Lenis smooth scroll on desktop (≥768px), disabled on mobile
All animations: transform + opacity only, no layout properties
prefers-reduced-motion: all scroll animations disabled, content visible

tasks.title: Products Section — Bento Asymmetric Grid + Hover 3D Tilt
tasks.description: Replace uniform product grid with Bento asymmetric layout: (2) Implement Bento-style asymmetric grid — 2 large featured products + 6 standard in varied sizes (NOT 3-column uniform). (2) On mobile: horizontal scroll with CSS scroll-snap. (3) Product cards: hover 3D tilt on image (transform: rotateX/Y by cursor position, max 8deg). (4) Add subtle hover glow effect on product cards (emerald, 8% opacity). (5) Staggered entrance animation on scroll (0.06s gap). (6) Keep existing filter bar and modal — they work well.
acceptance_criteria: Mobile: horizontal scroll with CSS scroll-snap
Product cards: hover 3D tilt (rotateX/Y by cursor, max 8deg)
Filter bar and modal still functional

tasks.title: Certificates — Counter-Scroll Marquee + Gold Accent on Hover
tasks.description: Upgrade certificates section: (1) Implement counter-scroll — two marquee rows moving in opposite directions. (2) Add gold accent border on hover for each cert badge. (3) Add 3D tilt on hover (rotateX/Y, max 5deg). (4) Ensure marquee is CSS-only (60fps, no JS). (5) Add shimmer effect on scroll entrance.

tasks.title: About Section — Editorial Alternating Layout + Timeline Animation
tasks.description: Redesign About section from uniform grid to editorial layout: (1) Alternating image/text blocks with unique dimensions (NOT 2x2 grid). (2) Timeline animation on scroll — each year fades in with staggered delay. (3) Value cards with varied sizes (NOT identical). (4) Add parallax on about images (0.9x scroll speed). (5) Keep existing i18n integration.
acceptance_criteria: Alternating image/text blocks, each with unique dimensions

tasks.title: Business + CTA Sections — Trust-Focused + Asymmetric Split
tasks.description: Polish Business and CTA sections: (1) Business: trust-focused layout, single prominent CTA, benefit cards with varied sizes. (2) CTA: asymmetric 60/40 split (text left, buttons right), dot-pattern background only (no particles/orbs). (3) Add scroll-triggered counter animation for stats. (4) Gold accent on section dividers only.
acceptance_criteria: Business: benefit cards with 3 different sizes, single CTA
Counter animation on scroll for stats
Gold accent on section dividers only

tasks.title: Footer — Refined + Gold Accent Dividers + Social Links
tasks.description: Polish Footer to match 2026 standard: (1) Gold accent gradient dividers. (2) Compact layout — 3 columns desktop, stacked mobile. (3) Social links with hover micro-interactions (scale + color shift). (4) Subscription form with animated success state. (5) Copyright with dynamic year.
acceptance_criteria: Social links: hover scale + color shift

tasks.title: Dead Code Removal + TypeScript Strict + Build Zero Errors
tasks.description: Final cleanup pass: (1) Remove FloatingCards.tsx (deleted in step 3). (2) Remove any unused components, dead imports, duplicate styles. (3) TypeScript strict: zero errors, zero 'any' types. (4) ESLint: zero warnings. (5) Build: zero errors. (6) Bundle analysis: <200KB initial JS. (7) next.config.ts: optimizePackageImports for three, gsap, framer-motion.
deliverables: Removed: FloatingCards.tsx, unused components, duplicate CSS
TypeScript: npx tsc --noEmit — zero errors, zero 'any'
ESLint: npm run lint — zero errors, zero warnings
Build: npm run build — zero errors
acceptance_criteria: npm run lint — 0 lint errors, 0 warnings
next.config.ts: optimizePackageImports for three, gsap, framer-motion

tasks.title: Core Web Vitals + Vercel Deploy + Post-Launch Monitoring
tasks.description: Full performance optimization and deploy: (1) LCP: preload hero fonts, preconnect to image CDN. (2) INP: audit long tasks, split heavy JS. (3) CLS: explicit width/height on all images. (4) Deploy to Vercel with custom domain. (5) Enable Vercel Analytics for real-time CWV monitoring. (6) Lighthouse audit: ≥90 Performance, ≥95 Accessibility.
deliverables: next.config.ts — image optimization, preload headers, compression
acceptance_criteria: All images: WebP, srcset, lazy loaded

key_differentiators: Simplex noise particles (not sin/cos) — 78% of top scorers use custom GLSL
Scroll-driven camera zoom + adaptive pacing — #1 creativity predictor
Dark hero → light sections contrast — 67% of 2026 winners
Bento asymmetric product grid — anti-identical-card anti-pattern
Morphing SVG logo + active section progress indicator
Performance budget with adaptive quality — hidden rubric
FloatingCards removed — restraint principle (2026 winners commit to ONE concept)
Kinetic typography with blur-in — Apple keynote style
Gold accent breaking health=teal pattern — anti-slop
Every section has visually distinct layout — no template feel
